title: [STP-FORESIGHT] — FAILURE ATLAS — 2026-03-10
author: Sheldon K. Salmon
subject: FAILURE ATLAS — Cross-domain failure node cartography repo
prediction: A cross-domain repository mapping structural gaps civilization repeats across centuries — named the Failure Atlas — will become a reference architecture cited by engineers, researchers, and institutions across at least three of its nine mapped domains. The adjacency principle it documents — that solutions to modern failure nodes already exist in adjacent domains and go uncarried — will be recognized as a named contribution to systems thinking.
reasoning: The Hebrew lunisolar calendar designates this era as the era of forgetting. Every domain audited during AV-UBI, Hyperloop v1-v3, and AGI specification work revealed the same pattern — prior solutions existed and were not carried forward. Floor 4 alone contains five rooms with documented ancient solutions to problems modern engineers rebuilt from scratch at enormous cost. The pattern is consistent across AI, medical, transportation, infrastructure, and legal domains. No single repository currently maps this cross-domain amnesia using sealed, falsifiable entries.
implication: FAILURE ATLAS becomes the first permanent cross-domain map of civilizational amnesia — a reference architecture that predates and outlasts any single domain's attempt to solve the same problem in isolation. Author of record: Sheldon K. Salmon. Sealed March 2026.
falsification_condition: If within 3 years no entry from any floor is cited, referenced, or engaged with by any researcher, engineer, or institution outside the author's direct network — the prediction is false.
confidence_level: Moderate — the work is structurally sound. Visibility timeline is uncertain.
stack: DUAL-HELIX v2.0 · TOPOS v0.3 · STP FROZEN-2.0
